problemStatement: Agent Olympus has strong design-review coverage but suffers from three concrete gaps: (1) generic LLM UI defaults that even Aphrodite cannot prevent without explicit negative constraints and project-specific brand context; (2) always-on reviewer overhead in Atlas/Athena that wastes 60-80% of reviewer tokens on diffs irrelevant to most agents; (3) brownfield stalls where finish-branch assumes a test harness exists and browser-based QA hits CAPTCHA/auth walls with no handoff path. A 3-way cross-review by Claude+Codex+Gemini against pbakaus/impeccable and garrytan/gstack reached unanimous consensus on five concrete adoptions plus five additional 2-of-3 consensus items.
targetUsers: Solo AI developers running /atlas or /athena on UI-heavy projects; small teams wanting design-system discipline auto-enforced; brownfield rescuers adopting Olympus on legacy repos with no tests.
goals: Eliminate generic LLM UI defaults via executable anti-pattern registry as a finish-branch gate
Make designer/aphrodite project-aware via /teach-design captured to .ao/state/design-identity.json and auto-injected through subagent-start hook

Replace monolithic design prompts with seven modular reference packs (typography, color-and-contrast, spatial-design, motion-design, interaction-design, responsive-design, ux-writing) lazy-loaded by ui-review
Add precision style-pass micro-skills (/normalize, /polish, /typeset, /arrange) for surgical UI iteration
Cut Athena reviewer overhead 40-80% via change-aware review router based on diff scope
Add browser handoff/resume so autonomous QA can pause for CAPTCHA/auth and continue from exact session state
Formalize cascade artifact pipe (.ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/) with strict inbox/outbox per orchestrator stage
Add deterministic frontend remediation chain (audit -> normalize -> polish -> harden) recorded in .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/
Add taste memory (.ao/state/taste.jsonl) for aesthetic preference accumulation distinct from wisdom
Add TTHW benchmarking so finish-branch tracks clone-to-running-tests latency
All existing tests continue to pass
Standalone operation without impeccable or gstack installed
nonGoals: Port impeccable's full 20-command surface (only 4 highest-leverage micro-skills)
Port gstack's /learn (wisdom covers it) or /autoplan (Atlas/Athena cover it)
Port gstack's GStack Browser binary or Pretext layout engine
Modify hook signatures or run.cjs entry point
Implement STRIDE security review or test bootstrap (deferred to v1.0.3)
Change Greek mythology agent persona structure

userStories.title: Anti-pattern registry + executable UI smell scan
userStories.iWantTo: have an automated scanner block merge if the diff introduces canonical LLM UI slop (Inter/system fonts, pure black, card-nesting, bounce easing, gray-on-color)
userStories.soThat: my project never ships visually-default AI output that all reviewers would call generic
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN config/design-blacklist.jsonc exists with anti-pattern rules WHEN ui-smell-scan.mjs runs THEN it loads the rules and reports each detection with file:line, rule id, and matched substring
GIVEN a known-bad seed fixture with all 5 canonical slop patterns WHEN scan runs THEN it detects at least 3 of the 5
GIVEN a project sets allowedFonts in design-identity.json WHEN scan encounters that font THEN it does not flag
GIVEN finish-branch runs WHEN ui-smell-scan finds violations THEN finish-branch fails with structured error listing all violations
GIVEN scan finds zero violations WHEN finish-branch continues THEN it logs the clean result to .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/

userStories.title: Modular design reference pack (7 lazy-loaded modules)
userStories.asA: designer or aphrodite agent reviewing UI
userStories.iWantTo: load only the relevant design reference module (typography, color-and-contrast, spatial-design, motion-design, interaction-design, responsive-design, ux-writing) for the current critique
userStories.soThat: my context window is not bloated by all 7 domains when only 1-2 apply
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN skills/ui-review/reference/ contains 7 markdown files (one per domain) WHEN ui-review skill runs THEN it parses the diff scope and loads only the relevant 1-3 modules
GIVEN a CSS-only diff WHEN ui-review runs THEN it loads color-and-contrast + spatial-design + responsive-design at minimum
GIVEN a copy-only diff (label/error message changes) WHEN ui-review runs THEN it loads only ux-writing
GIVEN no module match WHEN ui-review runs THEN it falls back to loading all 7 modules with a warning
GIVEN each reference module WHEN inspected THEN it contains: domain definition, top 5 principles, top 5 anti-patterns, 1 worked example

userStories.title: /teach-design identity briefing + auto-injection via subagent-start
userStories.asA: developer setting up Olympus on a new frontend project
userStories.iWantTo: run /teach-design once to capture brand colors, typography tokens, spacing scale, component library, and design conventions into .ao/state/design-identity.json
userStories.soThat: every subsequent designer/aphrodite/ui-review invocation references my project-specific taste rather than re-deriving generic 'good design'
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /teach-design is invoked WHEN the skill runs THEN it interviews the user (or auto-detects from package.json/tailwind.config/theme files) to populate brand colors, fonts, spacing tokens, and design conventions

GIVEN /teach-design completes WHEN saved THEN .ao/state/design-identity.json contains schema-validated fields: brand.colors, typography.fonts, spacing.scale, components.library, allowedFonts, conventions.notes
GIVEN design-identity.json exists WHEN subagent-start.mjs fires for designer/aphrodite/ui-review subagent THEN it injects the identity as additionalContext
GIVEN subagent without design-identity.json WHEN subagent-start fires THEN it injects nothing extra (no error)
GIVEN identity is updated WHEN /teach-design re-runs THEN existing fields are merged, not overwritten silently; user is shown the diff
GIVEN identity is corrupted WHEN subagent-start tries to load it THEN hook fails open (writes {}, exit 0) per existing fail-safe convention

userStories.title: Precision style-pass micro-skills (/normalize, /polish, /typeset, /arrange)
userStories.asA: developer iterating on a UI component
userStories.iWantTo: invoke a surgical micro-skill that fixes one specific concern (token alignment, final polish, typography only, layout/spacing only) without re-generating the whole component
userStories.soThat: I can iterate in 10% shifts instead of full redesigns, preserving layout stability
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /normalize is invoked on a target file or component WHEN it runs THEN it replaces hardcoded values with design tokens, leaves layout untouched, reports a diff summary
GIVEN /polish is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it applies final-pass refinements (alignment, spacing rhythm, micro-detail) without changing structure or copy
GIVEN /typeset is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it adjusts font choices, hierarchy, sizing, weight only — touches no other CSS
GIVEN /arrange is invoked WHEN it runs THEN it adjusts layout, spacing, visual rhythm only — touches no typography or color
GIVEN any micro-skill completes WHEN it finishes THEN it reports: files touched, lines changed, scope confirmation (e.g. '0 typography changes' for /arrange)
GIVEN any micro-skill is invoked WHEN it routes THEN it uses the designer agent at sonnet tier (not opus) for cost efficiency

userStories.title: Change-aware review router for Atlas/Athena reviewer fan-out
userStories.asA: Atlas or Athena orchestrator about to spawn reviewers
userStories.iWantTo: consult a router that maps the current diff scope to the minimal set of relevant reviewer agents
userStories.soThat: I do not waste 60-80% of reviewer tokens running architect/security on a CSS-only diff or aphrodite on a backend refactor
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN config/review-routing.jsonc contains diff-scope rules (frontend/backend/infra/docs/test/auth) WHEN review-router.mjs is called with a diff THEN it returns the list of reviewer agents to invoke
GIVEN a CSS-only diff WHEN router is called THEN it returns [aphrodite, designer] (not architect/security/code-reviewer)
GIVEN a backend-only diff WHEN router is called THEN it returns [code-reviewer, architect, security-reviewer] (not aphrodite/designer)
GIVEN an auth/security-touching diff (matches: jwt, session, password, oauth, cookie) WHEN router is called THEN it always includes security-reviewer regardless of other rules
GIVEN routing.alwaysInclude contains an agent WHEN router is called THEN that agent is always included
GIVEN router has no rule match WHEN called THEN it falls back to the full reviewer set with a warning logged
GIVEN Atlas Phase 4 verification runs WHEN router is consulted THEN reviewer fan-out uses only the returned set
GIVEN Athena worker integration runs WHEN router is consulted THEN cross-validation uses only the returned set

userStories.title: Browser handoff/resume protocol for autonomous QA
userStories.asA: developer running autonomous browser-based QA via Atlas/Athena
userStories.iWantTo: have the agent pause when it hits CAPTCHA/auth/MFA, hand off to a visible Chrome session for me to solve, and resume from the exact context
userStories.soThat: self-driving QA does not stall on real-world auth gates
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN agent encounters a recognized auth/captcha pattern (DOM markers: recaptcha, hcaptcha, 'verify you are human', login form) WHEN handoff is triggered THEN it persists session ID + current URL + breadcrumb to .ao/state/browser-handoff.json and emits a notification
GIVEN handoff state exists WHEN user runs /resume-handoff THEN agent reads the state, navigates to the persisted URL, and continues from the breadcrumb step
GIVEN handoff state is older than 24h WHEN /resume-handoff is invoked THEN it warns about staleness and asks for confirmation
GIVEN handoff persistence WHEN inspected THEN it contains NO cookies, headers, or credentials — only session ID, URL, and breadcrumb
GIVEN no browser provider available WHEN handoff is triggered THEN it logs the URL and instructs user to manually complete; never blocks indefinitely
GIVEN handoff resume succeeds WHEN orchestrator continues THEN the run artifact records both the pause and resume events

userStories.title: Cascade artifact pipe with strict inbox/outbox per orchestrator stage
userStories.asA: Atlas or Athena orchestrator running a multi-stage workflow
userStories.iWantTo: write each stage's outputs to .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/outbox/ and have the next stage read only from its inbox/
userStories.soThat: stages do not carry forward bloated history or hallucination from earlier prompts
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/artifact-pipe.mjs exposes writeOutbox(runId, stage, name, payload) and readInbox(runId, stage) WHEN orchestrator stages call these THEN files are written to .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stage>/{inbox,outbox}/
GIVEN stage N completes WHEN stage N+1 starts THEN it reads only from .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/<stageN+1>/inbox/, populated by symlinks/copies from stage N outbox
GIVEN a stage produces too much data WHEN written THEN payloads are capped at 100KB per file with a tail-truncation warning
GIVEN a run completes successfully WHEN cleanup runs THEN pipe directory is preserved for 24h (matching existing run-artifact retention) then deleted by SessionEnd
GIVEN Atlas/Athena are updated WHEN they spawn the next stage THEN they pass the inbox path explicitly in the prompt instead of dumping prior history
GIVEN a stage fails WHEN debugging THEN .ao/artifacts/pipe/<runId>/ is human-readable for postmortem

userStories.title: Sequential frontend remediation chain (audit -> normalize -> polish -> harden)
userStories.asA: developer who just received a ui-review report
userStories.iWantTo: invoke /ui-remediate to run a fixed audit -> normalize -> polish -> harden pipeline with each stage's result recorded
userStories.soThat: frontend fixes are deterministic and auditable instead of ad-hoc 'make it nicer' loops
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN /ui-remediate is invoked on a target WHEN it runs THEN it executes audit (ui-review), normalize, polish, harden in strict order
GIVEN any stage fails WHEN detected THEN the chain halts, records the failure, does not advance
GIVEN each stage completes WHEN the next starts THEN it receives only the prior stage's structured outbox payload (not full conversation history)
GIVEN the chain finishes WHEN it writes results THEN .ao/artifacts/runs/<runId>/ui-remediation.json contains: stage, status, files touched, time elapsed, summary for each step
GIVEN the chain is invoked from finish-branch WHEN integrated THEN finish-branch waits for chain completion before continuing
GIVEN the chain depends on US-004 micro-skills (/normalize, /polish) WHEN they are missing THEN /ui-remediate fails fast with a clear error

userStories.title: Taste memory — aesthetic preference accumulation distinct from wisdom
userStories.asA: developer who gives feedback like 'I prefer minimal layouts' or 'no rounded corners'
userStories.iWantTo: have those preferences accumulated in .ao/state/taste.jsonl and replayed to designer/aphrodite
userStories.soThat: my UI agent learns my taste over time without me re-stating it every session
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/taste-memory.mjs exposes recordTaste(entry) and loadTaste(limit) WHEN called THEN entries are appended to .ao/state/taste.jsonl in JSONL format
GIVEN a taste entry WHEN written THEN it contains: timestamp, source (user/auto), category (typography/color/layout/motion/copy), preference, anti-preference (optional)
GIVEN designer/aphrodite/ui-review subagent fires WHEN subagent-start.mjs runs THEN it injects the most recent 20 taste entries as additionalContext
GIVEN taste.jsonl exceeds 200 entries WHEN appended THEN oldest entries are pruned (FIFO) to stay under cap
GIVEN the user runs the sessions skill WHEN they request 'taste prune' THEN they can remove specific entries by ID or category
GIVEN taste-memory is corrupted WHEN loaded THEN function returns [] (fail-safe, never blocks)
GIVEN wisdom and taste are siblings WHEN inspected THEN they live in separate files; wisdom records facts, taste records aesthetics

userStories.title: TTHW (Time To Hello World) benchmarking on finish-branch
userStories.asA: developer or team lead tracking project devex health
userStories.iWantTo: have finish-branch optionally measure clone-to-first-passing-tests latency and append to .ao/state/tthw-history.jsonl
userStories.soThat: I can see if changes are introducing onboarding friction over time
acceptanceCriteria: GIVEN scripts/lib/tthw-bench.mjs exposes measureTTHW({repo, testCmd}) WHEN called THEN it clones to a temp dir, runs install + test command, reports total seconds
GIVEN finish-branch.skill is configured to run TTHW WHEN the branch is ready to merge THEN it triggers measurement async, never blocking the merge
GIVEN measureTTHW completes WHEN appended THEN .ao/state/tthw-history.jsonl contains: timestamp, branch, commit, seconds, install seconds, test seconds, status
GIVEN TTHW doubles vs the rolling 30-day median WHEN detected THEN finish-branch logs a warning (does not fail)
GIVEN TTHW measurement fails (network, missing test cmd) WHEN detected THEN it records the error and continues (fail-safe)
GIVEN tthw-history.jsonl exceeds 365 entries WHEN appended THEN oldest entries are pruned (FIFO)
GIVEN TTHW config is absent from autonomy.json WHEN finish-branch runs THEN it skips TTHW silently (opt-in)
